---
title: Audit
description: Callback hooks fired on every cache operation.
icon: ClipboardList
---

Fires callbacks after every `get`, `set`, and `delete`. Use for logging, metrics, alerting, or compliance tracing.

## Setup

```ts
import { auditPlugin } from "@justwant/cache/plugins/audit";
import { createCache } from "@justwant/cache";
import { memoryAdapter } from "@justwant/cache/adapters/memory";

const cache = createCache({
  adapter: memoryAdapter(),
  plugins: [
    auditPlugin({
      onGet: (key, hit) => console.log(`GET ${key} — ${hit ? "HIT" : "MISS"}`),
      onSet: (key) => console.log(`SET ${key}`),
      onDelete: (key) => console.log(`DELETE ${key}`),
    }),
  ],
});
```

## Options

| Option | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `onGet` | `(key: string, hit: boolean) => void \| Promise<void>` | Called after every `get`. `hit` is `true` if a value was found |
| `onSet` | `(key: string) => void \| Promise<void>` | Called after every `set` |
| `onDelete` | `(key: string) => void \| Promise<void>` | Called after every `delete` |

All callbacks are optional. Omit any you do not need.

## Notes

- Callbacks may be async — the plugin awaits them before returning.
- Errors thrown inside callbacks are **not caught** by the plugin. Handle them inside the callback if needed.
- All three options default to `undefined` — `auditPlugin()` is valid with no arguments.
